assembly_instructions: Open-source 1U CubeSat (10×10×10 cm). Four machined rails at the cube corners form the primary structural frame; thin end-caps close the top and bottom. A PC/104 stack of cards sits inside — battery card near the bottom, ADACS (attitude determination and control) module near the top, with a backplane PCB running along the back wall that connects them. Two deployable solar panels hinge outward from opposite side rails on small deploy mechanisms; a forward-facing imaging camera is mounted on one side wall, a sun sensor on the top face, and a communications monopole antenna extends from the top end-cap.

info.summary: Open-hardware 1U CubeSat from the Portland State Aerospace Society — a technology-demonstration precursor to the larger OreSat bus.
info.description: OreSat0 is an open-source 1U (10×10×10 cm) CubeSat developed by the Portland State Aerospace Society (PSAS). It was built to flight-qualify the structural, power, and command-and-data-handling systems of the larger OreSat bus before those subsystems flew on a 2U/3U science mission. The spacecraft follows classic CubeSat construction: four 6061 aluminum corner rails carry the launch loads, and a pair of thin end-caps close off the +Z and -Z faces. Inside, a PC/104-form-factor avionics stack holds a battery card, a backplane that carries the bus signals between cards, and an ADACS (Attitude Determination and Control) module. Two deployable solar panels hinge outward from opposite side rails via small spring-loaded burn-wire deploy mechanisms. Externally, the satellite carries a CMOS imaging camera, a sun sensor for coarse attitude, and a UHF monopole antenna. Structure and electronics are released under CERN-OHL-S v2.0.
info.contractor: PSAS student/volunteer team, Portland State University
info.status: Flown (deorbited). Design iterated into OreSat (2U+) flight bus.

facts.label: Power
facts.value: Body-mounted solar + two deployable panels → Li-ion battery card

parts.name: Corner Rail (Fwd-Right)
parts.role: Machined 6061 aluminum corner rail — one of four that carry launch-rail loads and bolt the end-caps together.

parts.name: Corner Rail (Fwd-Left)
parts.role: Forward-left corner rail (with deploy mechanism attachment).

parts.name: End Cap (Top, +Y)
parts.role: Top end-cap — seals the +Y face and mounts the antenna and sun sensor.

parts.name: End Cap (Bottom, -Y)
parts.role: Bottom end-cap — deployment-switch interface to the launch dispenser.

parts.name: Battery Card (PC/104)
parts.role: Li-ion battery board on the PC/104 stack — stores harvested solar power.

parts.name: PC/104 Backplane
parts.role: Vertical PC/104 backplane PCB along the rear interior wall; carries power and CAN bus between stacked cards.

parts.name: ADACS Module (PC/104)
parts.role: Attitude Determination and Control card — magnetometer, IMU, torque-rod drivers.

parts.name: Deployable Solar Panel (Port)
parts.role: Port-side deployable solar panel, hinged open 90° to catch sunlight. Recessed Si cells on one face.

parts.name: Deployable Solar Panel (Stbd)
parts.role: Starboard-side deployable solar panel (same design, mirrored).

parts.name: Imaging Camera
parts.role: Small CMOS imaging camera on the +Z face for technology-demonstration imagery.

parts.name: UHF Monopole Antenna
parts.role: Deployed UHF monopole, extending from the top end-cap after release.

parts.name: Burn-Wire Deploy Mechanism
parts.role: Spring-loaded hinge at the +Y edge of the port rail. Release is burn-wire actuated on deployment.

parts.name: Sun Sensor
parts.role: Photodiode-based coarse sun sensor on the +Y face; feeds ADACS.
